ai.ask.title: Prompt to AI

params.name: text
params.desc: Tell AI what you need. Use [_VAR.varname] to get a value from LLM. That value will be stored in [vars].

ai.ask.example: /ai.ask gemini what is the latest tech bubble?

ai.act.title: Tell AI what to do
ai.act.description: Ask AI to perform simple stuff for you in current tab. sLines fees apply.

params.name: text
params.desc: Tell AI what you want with current page. Be specific, eg. Remove that banner, open my dashboard.

ai.act.example: /ai.act gemini Login with abc@email.com and password abc

chrome.title: Open google chrome
chrome.description: Open a new window if not found, or bring active one front.

tab.title: Change/Open Tab
tab.description: Switch/set/open a specific tab. Call /store.tab newAlias to use the new tab in the script.

params.name: tabname
params.desc: Either tabs[] variable alias or new to open new tab. Doesn't work for direct terminal prompt.

params.name: flag
params.desc: -ifnotcurrent to change current tab, (ignore if already is) & -ifnotfound to check if any opened tabs has the url. Valid only when input is url.

params.name: input
params.desc: Tab number to switch to, url, or search query to Google

screenshot.title: Take Screenshot
screenshot.description: Capture a screenshot of the current tab. This command also allows DOM to update (eg. After click, url change, etc).

ss.save.title: Save Screenshot
ss.save.description: Save the last screenshot png to disk. It prompts user where to save the file.

ss.pixel.title: Get Pixel Color of Screenshot
ss.pixel.description: Return rgba, hex, and normalized rgba of last screenshot pixel

xpath.title: Get DOM XPath
xpath.description: Get XPath of element at specified coordinates
xpath.returns: XPath string of the element in the screenshot at the position
xpath.example: /xpath 0.3 0.4  - Get XPath of element at 30% width, 40% height

xpath.decode.title: Decode xpath
xpath.decode.description: Decode encoded xpath for tahcia purpose.

fetch.title: REST Call

params.name: method
params.desc: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)

params.name: body
params.desc: JSON body for POST/PUT, {} for GET

fetch.returns: Response data from the url. Set to $last.

elements.title: Get all elements
elements.description: Extract most useful elements in a the innerHTML
elements.returns: List of element, its normalized and absolute positions.

query.title: Get html content based on query
query.description: Use natural language to locate text in the page.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: Natural language description of what to locate in the screenshot

scroll.title: Scroll To DOM
scroll.description: Read text content at specified xpath/element

params.name: xOffset
params.desc: When set, X & Y should come together

params.name: yOffset
params.desc: When set, X & Y should come together

read.title: Read DOM
read.description: Read text content at specified xpath/element
read.returns: Text content of the element, or capture JSON of text arrays within common parent.

attr.title: Get DOM Attribute
attr.description: Read string attribute of DOM at specified xpath/element

property.title: Get DOM Property
property.description: Read property of DOM at specified xpath/element
property.returns: String of the property. Won't work with object.

el.center.title: Get Element Center Coordinates
el.center.description: Gets the center X and Y coordinates of a DOM element and stores them in variables

params.name: var_x
params.desc: Variable name to store X coordinate (center)

params.name: var_y
params.desc: Variable name to store Y coordinate (center)

el.center.returns: Sets variables with coordinates, returns empty string

el.top.title: Get Element Top Coordinate
el.top.description: Gets the top Y coordinate of a DOM element and stores it in a variable

params.name: var_y
params.desc: Variable name to store top Y coordinate

el.top.returns: Sets variable with top coordinate, returns the value

el.bottom.title: Get Element Bottom Coordinate
el.bottom.description: Gets the bottom Y coordinate of a DOM element and stores it in a variable

params.name: var_y
params.desc: Variable name to store bottom Y coordinate

el.bottom.returns: Sets variable with bottom coordinate, returns the value

el.width.title: Get Element Width
el.width.description: Gets the width of a DOM element and stores it in a variable
el.width.returns: Sets variable with width, returns the value

el.height.title: Get Element Height
el.height.description: Gets the height of a DOM element and stores it in a variable
el.height.returns: Sets variable with height, returns the value

el.left.title: Get Element Left Coordinate
el.left.description: Gets the left X coordinate of a DOM element and stores it in a variable

params.name: var_x
params.desc: Variable name to store left X coordinate

el.left.returns: Sets variable with left coordinate, returns the value

el.right.title: Get Element Right Coordinate
el.right.description: Gets the right X coordinate of a DOM element and stores it in a variable

params.name: var_x
params.desc: Variable name to store right X coordinate

el.right.returns: Sets variable with right coordinate, returns the value

fill.title: Fill input
fill.description: Fill a form input with text

params.name: selector
params.desc: XPath/CSS/Tahcia(:abc) selector of the input element

params.name: text
params.desc: Text to fill in the input

click.title: Click Element
click.description: Click on an element using CSS selector

fill.el.at.title: Fill DOM at position
fill.el.at.description: Fill element at x & y with text

scroll.el.at.title: Scroll Element at Position
scroll.el.at.description: Finds element at absolute screen coordinates (px,py), then scrolls that element by specified amounts

params.name: px
params.desc: Absolute X screen coordinate to find element

params.name: py
params.desc: Absolute Y screen coordinate to find element

params.name: scrollX
params.desc: Horizontal scroll amount (pixels). Use -1 for no horizontal scroll

params.name: scrollY
params.desc: Vertical scroll amount (pixels). Use -1 for no vertical scroll

source.title: Get HTML Source
source.description: Gets the current HTML source code of the active browser tab
source.returns: Returns the complete HTML source as a string

store.url.title: Store Current URL
store.url.description: Stores the current browser tab's URL into a variable

params.name: variable
params.desc: Variable name to store the URL

mac.mouse.drag.title: Mouse Drag
mac.mouse.drag.description: Tell OS to drag using mouse

mac.mouse.dblclick.title: Move Double Click
mac.mouse.dblclick.description: Tell OS to double click mouse

mac.open.title: Open App
mac.open.description: Launch a non-running desktop application using its bundle identifier string.
mac.open.returns: JSON string string with success status

mac.focus.title: Focus App
mac.focus.description: Bring an already running desktop application window into foreground focus.
mac.focus.returns: JSON string string with success status

mac.pixel.title: Get Pixel Color
mac.pixel.description: Capture RGBA, Hex, and normalized color data of a single screen pixel.
mac.pixel.returns: JSON containing rgba, hex, and normalized objects

mac.screenshot.title: Get desktop screenshot
mac.screenshot.description: Capture mac screenshot. CLI installation required.

mac.ocr.title: Screen OCR
mac.ocr.description: Perform screen-wide text recognition to extract all text blocks and center point coordinates.
mac.ocr.returns: Array of JSON objects with text, coordinates, scale, and bounding boxes

mac.windows.title: List Windows
mac.windows.description: Retrieve an array list of all currently open and visible window objects.
mac.windows.returns: Array of JSON objects containing bundleId and bound specifications

mac.key.shortcut.title: Execute Keyboard Shortcut
mac.key.shortcut.description: Simultaneously holds modifier flags and hits targeted terminal keys.

params.name: keys
params.desc: Space-separated sequence of keys (e.g. 'cmd shift q')

copy.title: Copy from Element
copy.description: Reads the text content of a DOM element, optionally extracts a substring via regex, and writes it to the clipboard.

params.name: selector
params.desc: XPath or CSS selector of the source element

params.name: regex
params.desc: Regex pattern to extract a sub-string. Capture group 1 is preferred, else full match.

paste.title: Paste into Element
paste.description: Pastes current clipboard into a target element with index and or regex.

params.name: selector
params.desc: XPath/CSS/Tahcia(:abc) selector of the target element

params.name: index
params.desc: Index where cursor is in input, used only when regex fails.

hover.title: Hover Element
hover.description: Hover on an element using CSS selector

point.title: Point a position with dot
point.description: Hover on an element using CSS selector

note.title: Show Note Popup On Tab
note.description: Show commands run and or custom message on controlled tabs.

echo.title: Say something
echo.description: Echo text back to the console
echo.returns: The same text that was input

store.title: Store Last Result
store.description: Save the last command's result into a named variable.

params.name: varname
params.desc: Variable name to store the last result into.

store.tab.title: Store Last Tab Id
store.tab.description: Save the last tab operation's result's tabId into an alias.

params.name: varname
params.desc: Tab alias to store the last tabId into.

extract.title: Extract from JSON Array
extract.description: Traverse a JSON array variable using an index pattern. Use -1 as a wildcard to match every element at that depth.

params.name: varname
params.desc: Variable name holding a JSON array string.

params.name: pattern
params.desc: Index path as a JSON array. -1 means every element at that depth. e.g. [0,-1,2]

extract.returns: JSON array of matched string values. Also sets $last.

pattern.title: Detect Extraction Pattern
pattern.description: Given a JSON array variable and sample strings, auto-detects the index pattern needed to extract them. Use the result with /extract.

params.name: varname
params.desc: Variable name holding a JSON array string.

params.name: samples
params.desc: One or more substrings to locate within the array. The command finds their shared path pattern.

pattern.returns: JSON pattern array usable with /extract. eg. [-1][0][5][-1][0]. Also sets $last.

ask.title: Ask for answer
ask.description: Ask a question and get a response
ask.returns: An predefined answer to the question
ask.example: /ask "Paris/New York" "What is the capital of France?"

cmp.title: Compare values
cmp.description: Compare two values using operators. For run script only.

params.name: operator
params.desc: Comparison operator (==, <> (number), != (string), >, <, >=, <=, includes (substr), in (string in array))

csv.import.title: Import CSV File
csv.import.description: Opens a file picker, reads the selected CSV file, and stores it as a 2D JSON array (array of rows, each row is an array of cell strings). The first row is included as-is (treat as headers if needed).

params.name: varname
params.desc: Variable name to store the resulting 2D JSON array.

arr.get.title: Get Element
arr.get.description: Appends a value to the end of a JSON array stored in a variable.

params.name: varname1
params.desc: Variable name holding a JSON array.

arr.get.returns: Updated JSON array string. Also sets $last.

arr.push.title: Array Push
arr.push.description: Appends a value to the end of a JSON array stored in a variable.

params.name: varname1
params.desc: Variable name holding a JSON array.

arr.push.returns: Updated JSON array string. Also sets $last.

arr.pop.title: Array Pop
arr.pop.description: Removes and returns the last element of a JSON array stored in a variable.

params.name: varname
params.desc: Variable name holding a JSON array.

arr.len.title: Array Length
arr.len.description: Returns the length of a JSON array and stores it in a variable.

params.name: varname
params.desc: Variable name to store the length.

params.name: jsonVar
params.desc: Variable name contaning a JSON array.

record.title: Record actions across tabs
record.description: Record mouse actions, input, and save the actions as [scriptname]

params.name: action
params.desc: Either start, stop, pause, or continue

params.name: scriptname
params.desc: Only needed for 'start'. New script name saved when stopped.

chromeoffset.title: Set Toolbar+Bookmark height
chromeoffset.description: Set Y mouse offset in chrome

params.name: y
params.desc: y in pixel, offset from top of chrome window to visible html

run.title: Run custom script
run.description: Execute your custom script from the library.

params.name: script
params.desc: Script identifier in format @owner/name. Use @me/name to use your own script.

params.name: flag
params.desc: -debug flag starts the script step by step

params.name: tabId
params.desc: #TabId (found above the screenshot) to register as the script's tabs:{...} require, in the same order. Var TAB.yourTabName.URL can be set to avoid manual tabId params, in which case, Tahcia will look for connected tab with the exact URL.

fork.title: Fork Script
fork.description: Copy any public or your other script.

params.name: script
params.desc: Script identifier in format @owner/name. Use @me/name to use your own script.

schedule.title: Schedule Script
schedule.description: Set or clear a schedule for a script. Schedule format: @*[09:00] (daily), @mon[09:00] (weekly every Mon), mon[09:00] (once), @m[30] (every 30 min), m[5] (run once after 5 min).

wait.element.found.title: Wait for element to show up

wait.key.down.title: Wait for key down
wait.key.down.example: /wait.key.down escape shift ctrl | wait.key.down P cmd ctrl

for.title: For loop

params.name: varName
params.desc: Like that in for (varName=....;;). Accessible via vars.

params.name: syntax
params.desc: Tahcia commands, must start with / and separated by ;

for.example: /func x 1 5 { /func y $x 5 { /echo $x$y; /store printed; /func q data['yes'] = data.printed.includes('4'); return data; } }

func.title: Run javascript function to modify vars

params.name: varName
params.desc: returned string value from code will be set to vars.varName. If you return an object, vars = returned value. Be careful.

params.name: code
params.desc: Javascript code (like in a function(data)) that call returns at the end. Do not use " ` characters

func.example: /func retVar var x = ""; for (var i=0;i<10;i++) x+=data.abc; return x;
